Name: Steve's
Description: Casual diner that has the BEST Club Sandwich and Fried Zuchinni. Would also recommend the French Toast Platter. Crispy Diet Coke.

Name: Din Tai Fung
Description: Shangnai Chinese cuisine with a great atmosphere. Pork XLB, Spicy Wontons, Shanghai Rice Cakes with Chicken, skip the Short Ribs but go for the Spicy Peanut Noodles and the Green Beans. Crispy Diet Coke!

Name: El Burrito Junior
Description: Yes, it's a chain, but this is the location is the best. Breakfast is superior here, but go past ~9am in order for the beans to be the best. No Diet Coke.

Name: Riviera Mexican
Address: 1615 S Pacific Coast Hwy, Redondo Beach, CA 90277
Description: Thin chips, delicious salsa. Baja Fish Tacos are AMAZING and so are their Fajitas.

Name: Original Pancake House
Address: 1756 S Pacific Coast Hwy, Redondo Beach, CA 90277
Description: Best location I've evber been to. For parties of two- split the Ham and Cheese Omlette with a cheddar-jack mix and an extra side of pancakes. Be aware of long lines on the weekends. No reservations, waitlist only. Diet Coke in a can.

Name: The San Franciscan
Description: A South Bay staple. Pork Chop is tasty, but it's more about the vibes. If you go on the right days, there's a live band and it really feels like you've stepped into the 1970s. Cosmo martinis are spectacular, but take an Uber.

Name: Fete Bistro by Slay
Description: Steak frites are fresh and crispy, Moules en Frites are a must-order. Great selection of French wine, and craft cocktails were very well-made.

Name: X Speakeasy
Description: Great place for a drink after dinner. On a Thursday, it'll be a little empty but all the better to actually talk to the people you're with.

Name: Musso and Frank's
Description: A Hollywood institution for a reason. Waiters still wear bow-ties and will tell you whose booth you're sitting at when you get sat. I don't even care if they were telling the truth or not. Go on a Thursday for the Chicken Pot Pie or a Saturday for a can't-miss Short Rib. Lamb is also incredible. Surprisingly, so is the Rigatoni with Lamb Ragut. Take an Uber- cocktails are strong and wine is too good to put down.

Name: Baja Sharkeez
Attire: Jeans and a going out top
Description: I've never eaten the food here, but I can say with absolute certainty this is a spot for the >30 crowd. Go with a friend and get the Shark Attack and you can repurpose the bucket as a flower vase.

Name: The Strand House
Address: 117 Manhattan Beach Blvd, Manhattan Beach, CA 90266
Description: Citrus Ahi Tuna Salad, Fiedo di Mare, Double Cut Lamb Chops, Parker House Rolls. You won't have room for dessert, but order the Glazed Donuts anyway. One bite won't kill you, but the regret of not having them might.

Name: The Penthouse
Description: Tuna crispy rice is perfect for an appetizer, but it might only be available for lunch. During a clear day, you can see the whole beach and the water is gorgeous, and at night the skyline is incredibly gorgeous. Duck L'Orange is beautifully presented, have an espresso martini for dessert instead of the souflee- I've been tricked before.

Name: Tower 12
Attire: Jeans and a going out top
Description: Never eaten here either, but this is the place that I run into everyone from highschool at. Every time, regardless of time of year. Something special about their Cosmo, but too many might have you asking, "Why didn't we date again?" to the people you really should not be asking that.

Name: Redondo Beach Brewing Company
Address: 1814 S Catalina Ave, Redondo Beach, CA 90277
Description: Great lunch spot, especially if it's warm enough to eat outside. Chinese chicken salad, tossed in the kitchen, with extra dressing on the side and a crispy Diet Coke and the sunshine helps make it the perfect place to have a pit stop while walking around the Riviera.

Name: Rebel Republic Social House
Address: 1710 S Catalina Ave, Redondo Beach, CA 90277
Description: Never eaten at this spot either, but their seasonal cocktail menu is always fun and the most interesting people in Redondo tend to congregate here. Two drink max- otherwise the price on your tab might shock you.

Name: Riviera House
Address: 1708 S Catalina Ave, Redondo Beach, CA 90277
Description: Start with a Scarlet Letter or a Killjoy, share the Poke Nachos or the Coconut Shrimp and try the Fish and Chips. No need for dessert here, or throw in a third app (Coconut Curry Mussels- yum!) and keep it light! Great patio space and beachfront vibes.

Name: Rockefeller
Address: 1707 S Catalina Ave, Redondo Beach, CA 90277
Description: There's a reason The Obvious Choice is named that. A bright, fresh, subtly sweet cocktail starts the perfect background to some fabulous Ahi Wonton Nachos that are individually put together (this is important to me specifically so I'm including it). If you're there for lunch, the Healthy Steak Salad is light and refreshing, but for dinner he Crispy Chicken Sandwich is incredible with a side of the Truffle Fries. As far as their wine selection, I've gotta reccomend a glass of the Daou Sauvvy B with any of the above.

Name: Good Stuff
Address: 550 Deep Valley Dr #151, Rolling Hills Estates, CA 90274
Description: A chain of diners around the South Bay, it just depends where you are in the area. This location is usually the least full, and there's a nice scenic drive up there. For breakfast, the California Quesadilla is great, and the Loco Moco is one of the best outside of Hawaii I've ever had. For lunch, the Chase's Fried Chicken wrap with Fries is INCREDIBLE and somehow magically gluten free if you're into that sort of thing.

Name: Nelson's
Address: 100 Terranea Way, Rancho Palos Verdes, CA 90275
Description: The Ceviche or the Ahi Poke Bowl are so fresh and if you're lucky enough to be there during whale watching season they feel like the bait that's drawing them closer. The Fish and Chips are fresh (the oil tastes clean which is another weird thing to say but it's important) but the Spicy Chicken Sandwich is also a big hit (I get no cheese). Grab a glass of Whispering Angel (or two!) and enjoy the cliffside view.

Name: Mar'Sel
Address: 100 Terranea Way, Rancho Palos Verdes, CA 90275
Description: Before a dinner here, I had never had a homemade English Muffin. It changed my life- and that's just the free bread. The burrata was incredible, the Carpaccio even better, and the Porcini risotto was a little unmemorable. I'd trade that in for the Ragu di "Carni Bianche" which I only had one bite of but I wanted more. The Pavlova is an incredible end to a meal there, and the atmosphere elegant and refined.

Name: The Hare
Attire: Jeans and a going out top
Description: I'd call this more of a dive bar than anywhere else on this list, but it's a great place to end the night. Floors are slightly sticky, clientele is all locals, but it's a neighborhood type of place. Nothing immensely special, but a place where you'll still feel at home.

Name: Saint & Second
Description: Bone marrow. Parker House Rolls. French Onion Soup. 100 Layer Lasagna. You'll have to roll out of there. Fun on a weeknight, definitely get a reservation either way, the wait can be brutal. And for good reason

Name: The Second Owl
Description: The Kao Soi curry from here still haunts my dreams. It's silky, spicy, and the meat in it is tender. It's truly a fabulous place.

Name: Marlena
Address: 5854 E Naples Plaza, Long Beach, CA 90803
Description: Start with the Bianco bread, but the pizzas are the star of the show. My top two are definitely the spicy salami and the blue oyster mushroom- the crust is thin, there's a little bit of char on the crust, and it compliments the atmosphere perfectly. If you MUST have something green then get some of the olives from the starter menu. I have it on good authority the olive oil is great to dip the bread into, but I can't vouch because I don't like olives.

Name: BOA
Description: I've said it once, and I'll say it again: I'm a sucker for a Parker House Roll, and these are some of the best. The Hamachi Crudo is so fresh and clean, and a hidden gem would definitely be the Goat Cheese Baklava. When I was here, we did family-style dining with a couple of their steaks, but by far the best was the 6666 Ribeye. It's some sort of special collaboration but it is incredible. The wagyu was the same as any other steakhouse, but the twice baked potato with caviar was a little too rich for my blood. I'd drift toward a steak hedre, but I'm sure that the seafood is good too.

Name: Eddie V's
Description: I'm a sucker for a mule of any sort, so please believe me when  say that the Pomegranate Mule is a spectacular feat. Because I'm picky, I get it with Grey Goose, but if you like Tito's I'm sure that's good too. This is one of the only places I'll get oysters on the half shell, but it's because their cocktail sauce is so incredible. The oysters are basically a vessel for it because it's so good. I'd encourage splitting the Bone-In Ribeye with the Au Gratin Potatoes and the Grilled Asparagus and do your best to not pop a button on your pants.

Name: Flemming's
Description: The crab cakes are absolutely to die for, and so is the Carpaccio. Something about the sauce drizzled on the top just melts with the thin beef in your mouth and you ascend to the heavens. I'd recommend wholeheartedly getting the garlic butter mushroom topper with whatever steak you choose, because it makes it entirely succulent and earthy. The Short Rib is rich and perfect for the winter, but keep in mind that richness because you'll need to save room for one of the most fabulous glasses of Sauterns at the end (Chateau Climens just in case you get lost).

Name: Sugarfish
Description: Be adventerous. Go for the "Don't think. Just Eat. Trust Me." and let it take you on the most spectacular omakase journey at one of the most reasonable prices I've seen in a while. Not for the faint of heart though, as I was exposed to fresh uni for the first time here and while that particular piece I would not enjoy again I'm sure other people will like it.

Name: Crustaean
Address: 468 N Bedford Dr, Beverly Hills, CA 90210
Description: Completely bypass half the menu and head for the secret section at the bottom. Ask your server very nicely to do an order of the crab out of the shell WITH the famous garlic noodles. Acompany it with the Wagyu Fried Rice (the pineapple is so pleasant and tart) and the Ginger Sea Bass. Highly reccomend the incredible Coconut Margarita with a black salt rim- it had some sort of fancy name that's escaping me right now.

Name: CUT
Description: There are a few of these too, but I've never had as much fun at them as I have at this location. Maybe it's the staff, maybe it was the company, but this is a CUT above the rest (ha get it) and especially convienent if you're staying in the Beverly Wilshire Hotel, where it's located. Or, if you're just feeling boujee.

Name: Bacari
Description: There's too many good things on this menu to just point to a few things, but I will anyway. The Lamb Hummus is incredible, as are the Shawarma Tacos, but one of the best is the street corn. If it's warm enough to sit outside, go for it. The people watching is out of this world.

Name: Mastro's Ocean Club
Address: 246 N Canon Dr, Beverly Hills, CA 90210
Description: There's a handful of these, but this location is only second to the location in Las Vegas. The Sea Bass might be the best thing on the menu because it's so simply done, there's not a fancy sauce to hide behind, just the crisp taste of the fish and the buttery texture of the sea bass in the first place. The Butter Cake is worth getting, but it is very rich at the end of the meal and unfortunately does not travel very well.

Name: Circa 55
Description: I'd recommend this place for their clean breakfast smoothies, juices, and health-food options. It feels like somewhere out of a wellness retreat (with a stunning view) but their pancakes are to die for. Get all the toppings on the side though, beause it's too much alltogther. But, share them with the table if you need something savory too.

Name: Bar Noir
Address: 140 S Lasky Dr, Beverly Hills, CA 90212
Attire: Jeans and a going out top
Description: This is one of the best-kept industry secret bars. It's a quieter place to go, in a boutique hotel in Beverly Hills, and a very popular spot for wrap parties for the studios down the way. It's dark, moody, and the drinks are very well crafted. They're also one of the only bars that I know of that does an absinthe tasting, which is a perfect "I did it once" type of experience!

Name: Tartine
Description: It's rare that I find a good club sandwich ouside of a diner, especially one at a place that looks as pretty as Tartine. The Turkey Club is somehow out of this world, and it's honestly even better for breakfast if you add an egg. This would by no means be a light meal, though it might if you end up with one of the salads that I'm sure is tasty enough.

Name: Nobu
Description: Yes, I know this place is for the influencers. Go during lunch when the view of the beach and the water is at its peak, and you won't regret it. Go for the Yellowtail and Jalapeno, skip the Toro Tartare, but trade it in for the Seaweed Salad. The Spicy Tuna Crispy Rice will not be presented in the way you would expect, for better or for worse, but the Wagyu Tacos exceed expectations. The Black Cod is a staple for a reason, and the Affogato with specifically the Black Honey Gelato is incredibly memorable. Make sure the phone eats too- you've gotta fit in somewhere.

Name: Big Nick's
Address: 1110 N Gaffey St, San Pedro, CA 90731
Description: This is the perfect pizza place for takeout, but if you're going to get anything else then I'd eat in. It doesn't usually travel well. The BBQ Chicken pizza has one of the best thin BBQ sauces that's sweet, vinegary, and just a little spicy but it soaks into the crust for one of the most tantalizing experiences. My dad vouches for their meat-lovers pizza, but somehow I never get to have a piece of that.

Name: Golden Pheasant
Description: Currently, this spot is temporarily closed. However, if it's after March 9th, then make a beeline for this place before everyone else realizes it's open again. Their wonton soup is the most comforting thing that I've ever eaten, and the Mongolian Beef is so thinly sliced it's almost paper thin. If you must have something green, the Sauteed Chicken with String Beans has a deliciously garlicky and tangy sauce. If noodles are more your thing, the BBQ Pork Pan Fried Noodle is mouth-watering. The menu is like the Cheesecake Factory- it's pages and pages of things that are all delicious. The only thing I'd not get tricked by is the Sizzling Rice Soup, because the rice just gets soggy after a while.

Name: Totoyama Sushi
Address: 6201 Hollywood Blvd Suite 118, Los Angeles, CA 90028
Description: This is an intimate, warm sushi restaurant. The chef is right there, and will tell you about anything you want to hear. If the place is empty enough, you can ask him about his early days and there's a few interesting stories there. The sushi is fresh, clean, and all very well put together. It's a short walk to the Hollywood Pantages Theatre, and if you're catching a matinee it's the perfect prequel. Make a reservation there though, just in case.

Name: Slice and Pint
Address: 601 N Pacific Coast Hwy, Redondo Beach, CA 90277
Description: I love that you can get a couple slices and a pint here for a reasonable price. They have interesting rotating selection that you can get individually, but they also have a curated list of phenomenal pizzas that changes every day. The beer here is all from local breweries around Redondo, El Segundo, and Manhattan Beach. It's a truly local place that has high ceilings and a little bit of an industrial vibe. Plus- consistent 80s music.

Name: Yum Thai Bistro
Description: This place is usually a prequel to the ice cream shop next door (see the Handel's description) but it is the perfect overture. Light, fresh Thai food with kind service and some of the best fried tofu as an appetizer. The Tom Kha is velvety yet still light from the bright lemongrass and lime juice, and the Pad Thai is the first one I've ever had and is by default the one I measure all the rest against. Don't fill all the way up though, Handel's is next door.

Name: Handel's Ice Cream
Address: 1882 S Pacific Coast Hwy, Redondo Beach, CA 90277
Description: I can't eat any other ice cream without wishing it was from this place. It's fresh, dense, and they do not skimp on the toppings. When I'm in the mood to chew my ice cream, I gravitate to the Oree-Dough, which has chocolate chip cookie dough and Oreo pieces scattered throughout a creamy vanilla base. However, if I just want to sit there and enjoy a scoop on a cone,  then I'd push you toward their cake batter flavor. It's exactly like yellow cake batter, but without the risk of salmonella. The list of flavors is a mile long, and there's something for everyone there.

Name: Ko-Ryu Ramen
Address: 24631 Crenshaw Blvd unit K, Torrance, CA 90505
Description: A little chill in the breeze? Head down to one of their two locations to ward it off. Their Koi Ramen is incredibly rich that warms you from the inside out because they use both chicken and pork in this tonkotsu-style broth. I like to get it with bamboo shoots for that texture, but there's a variety of additional toppings that can personalize the bowl to your taste. The gyoza appetizer is a great starter, and you can tell that they're made in house. The way they package it too is well-designed and travels well back to wherever you're staying.

Name: Ginza AYCE Sushi
Description: A great casual, neighborhood sushi spot that feels pretty authentic. This isn't the type of place to go to for omakase, but their specialty rolls are fresh and some have unique flavor combinations that have influence from all over the world. Conviently located and a good spur-of-the-moment place when you need a bite!

Name: Bonnello's
Description: Nestled in Downtown Lomita, this pizza place has been there for as long as anyone can remember. Their Sicillian slices are the perfect blend of crispy, thick, with a rich tomato sauce that can only be from San Marzano tomatoes. The windows have paint drawings all over that are chipped, and the photographs are faded, but this place isn't going anywhere anytime soon.

Name: Corridor Flow
Description: Of the pretentious coffee shops in Los Angeles, this is one of the best. Yes, they have the seven-dollar lattes, but the Cereal Latte is one of the best. Their bagel and lox isn't bad either- but their Avodaco Toast is absolutely killer. Their matcha is pretty good, but I'd stick to the coffee.

Name: Hi-Fi Espresso
Address: 1815 S Catalina Ave, Redondo Beach, CA 90277
Description: I love this place! Especially, I love the Bees Knees latte- the hot honey is such a pleasant surprise and somehow works. This is strange for me, because I don't tend to like spicy drinks but this is an exception. The French Toast Latte is also very pleasant- the cinnamon and maple work in harmony with the bitter coffee. Theres a few locations, but I like this one the best.

Name: Yellow Vase
Address: 31248 Palos Verdes Dr W, Rancho Palos Verdes, CA 90275
Description: There's several locations, but this one has the best view. I prefer their sweet crepes to the savory options, but that's true for any crepe. It's super fast and tasty, but don't get tricked by the quiches in the window. The crepes are much better, and so is the Croque Madame. Their lattes are pleasant, but nothing to write home about.

Name: Avenue Italy
Address: 31243 Palos Verdes Dr W, Rancho Palos Verdes, CA 90275
Description: This is a great Italian place with a gorgeous view and fresh ingredients. Pro tip- pull up the menu on your phone and get a Bruschetta for the table for free! The Rigatoni con Luganega is perfect, but the Spaghetti Aglio, Olio, e Peperocino is better somehow. It's the type of dish that you go home and try to recreate for years but with no avail.

Name: The Crepery Café
Address: 810 Silver Spur Rd, Rolling Hills Estates, CA 90274
Description: As the name suggests, the crepes from here are pretty good, but surprisingly so are the Chilaquiles. Wednesday mornings around 8-9 there'll be a huge rush of highschool students, but every other time it would be an easy breakfast spot to pop into!

Name: Dragonfly Thai
Address: 50-A Peninsula Center, Rolling Hills Estates, CA 90274
Description: A neighborhood place that has some of the best Pad Thai and Pad Kra Pow that I've had outside of a friends' moms' kitchen. It's good portions, relatively inexpensive, but I'd go after the high school lunch rush. It's walking distance, and there gets to be a bit of a rush.

Name: Pho Hana
Address: 55-B Peninsula Center, Rolling Hills Estates, CA 90274
Description: All through middle and high school, this was the place we went to after all the important things. A bowl of pho from here is nostalgic, the bulgogi is enuogh to share, and if you're just running in then the boba is can't miss.

Name: Lunada Market and Deli
Address: 2201 Palos Verdes Dr W, Palos Verdes Estates, CA 90274
Description: The smoothies here might be the best thing on their menu. I mostly came here to see my friends who were working on weekends, but it was the perfect pre-walk drink. The deli here also has great cuts of meat for grilling, and some are marinaded even!

Name: Seaside Bistro
Address: 1420 W 25th St Unit A, San Pedro, CA 90732
Description: This is a perfect little bistro place where basically the whole menu is good. You'll probably run into someone's parents you know, so be on your best behavior. The clientele is almost exclusively locals, and if you go early enough half of them will be longshoremen. Their fries are probably my favorite part- they're crispy, pillowy. and thick enough to hold nicely onto some ranch but not so thick your mouth runs dry.

Name: Sorrento's
Address: 2428 S Western Ave, San Pedro, CA 90732
Description: This is the type of place the Little League team comes to for celebratory pizzas after winning their games. It's warm, bursting with life, and the perfect neighborhood Italian spot. Get the Baked Rigatoni or the Chicken Parmasean- and if you eat in they both come with the world's best side salads.

Name: Perry's
Address: 2400 Ocean Front Walk, Santa Monica, CA 90405
Description: Perry's has a handful of stands up and down the beaches, and they're all tasty but this one has the best people watching. It's the perfect spot for a burger on the beach while you're still warm from the sun, though it can be a little pricey. This one is more of a restaurant, while most of the other locations are more about walkikng up and grabbing a hot dog. The hot dogs are amazing too, though sadly no Diet Coke.

Name: Fox & Farrow
Description: Any place that has a Pretzel Loaf as the first thing on the menu is an automatic win in my book, but it only gets better from there. But, don't get tricked by the first instance, and instead bypass it for focusing on the Sausage Selection, which changes all the time, which still incudes the pretzel loaf but with a fantastic selection of meats.  Without a doubt, the best thing I had was the Duck Pasta, hold te pine nuts. I still think about it- the shape of the pasta, the richness of the herb reduction... Yum.

Name: Frida
Address: 21438 Del Amo Cir N, Torrance, CA 90503
Description: It's not often that I find enchiladas that are as good as the ones from my roommate's abuela, but these are a close second. The mole is decadent, spicy, and just the right amount of bitter that you need it to be. The vibes are high, the chips are hot, and the margaritas are dangerous. Take an Uber.

Name: 555 East
Address: 555 E Ocean Blvd, Long Beach, CA 90802
Description: Baked goat cheese, shrimp cocktail, I could go on and on. This is a classic steakhouse that guarentees their experience unconditionally. They have good legs to stand on, speaking absoolutely. You step back into 1957 when you walk in, and it's the most pleasant experience. Best shared with friends, their steaks are their pride and joy for a reason. Their daily cuts change (daily... duh) but give them a shot if you're feeling that type of dry-aged vibe.

Name: Chart House
Address: 231 Yacht Club Way, Redondo Beach, CA 90277
Description: This is somewhere I wouldn't say to go unless it was a specific occasion. They host wine dinners regularly, and the experience is like no other. It feels top of the line, because it is, and you'll end up with an experience that can't be replicated or duplicated.

Name: Hudson House
Description: This place is somewhere I would be sure to grab a reservation at! It's always busy, and for good reason. It reminds me of Houston's where they serve incredibly good sushi but also great burgers. Their Lemon Drop martini is to die for (I think it's the limoncello foam) and they have Disaronno for an after-dinner cocktail. Great weeknight spot!

Name: Maria's
Address: 1712 S University Dr, Fort Worth, TX 76107
Description: It's hard not to rave about their margaritas and their empanadas, so that's what I'll do. The atmosphere is lively, the staff is personable, and the chips and salsa are incredible. A note about the parking- there is a lot on site, but there are not a lot of spots, so I would reccomend carpooling or taking and Uber.

Name: Birdie's Social Club
Attire: Jeans and a going out top
Description: Birdie's is the perfect place to sit on a warm summer afternoon. Staff is so kind and friendly, and if you get hungry before 9pm you can order Blue Mesa Mexican to your table. It's like magiv. They have two event venues, and local music is a frequent flier. During the holidays it's decked to the nines and they have a curated cocktail menu a mile long.

Name: Terry Black's BBQ
Description: Incredible, yet expensive. Would recommend going in a group large enough that you can get one of the bigger platters, because you can always take leftovers home. Their jalapeno and cheddar sausage is life-changing, and surprisingly their fresh green beans are a crowd-pleaser. Can't reccomend the corn enough either.

Name: Wabi House
Description: Warm, comforting, yet elevated. This is a geat take on ramen and the accompanying various acoutrements that follow. The gyoza are a great appetizer, and each dish is almost fully customizeable. If you're not going for lunch, then be sure to make a reservation or be prepared to wait in line. There's usually an electronic waitlist that's pretty accurate!

Name: Maggie's R&R
Address: 1264 W Magnolia Ave, Fort Worth, TX 76104
Description: Happy hour central with a side of live music and great vibes. I love this patio as soon as the weather turns warm, and so does the rest of Fort Worth. You can usually grab a table (as long as your party isn't too big) and I would definitely recommend the Dirty Shirleys. They do it with half ginger beer and half Sprite, so there's a refreshing extra bite to every sip/

Name: Maison Sauvage
Attire: Jeans and a going out top
Description: This bar and café (but mostly bar) was around the corner from our hotel, and I think we went every night for a pre-dinner cocktail. It is France, so be aware of slow service, but it's adorable

Name: Jules Verne
Address: Avenue Gustave Eiffel 2ème, Eiffel Tower, Av. Anatole France, 75007 Paris, France
Description: Eating here was the first time I had ever been to a Michelin Star restaurant. And my goodness, was it worth it. If not for the fact that the butter pats were monogrammed but also that the entire staff was attentive, professional, and very apt. Of course, you're paying for the service, but it is something that you know in advance. Upscale French dining, be aware of your limitations when it comes to that. Fois gras foam is not for the faint of heart.

Name: Chez Bartolo
Description: Yes, I'm reccomending an italian place that's in France. First of all, they have not just one but two barrolos on the menu last time I was there, and their pastas and pizzas are incredible. It's the perfect place for family style dining, and just… fun. Perfect for breaking up the monotony that French food sometimes has. Second of all, it's consistent. We'e been twice in two years and had the exact same fabulous experience both times.

Name: Flower Child
Address: 1616 S University Dr #301, Fort Worth, TX 76107
Description: Perfect for lunch on a weekday, and has a handy-dandy order-ahead feature. The mac-and-cheese is famous for a reason, but not enough love is given to the cauliflower risotto. I've never had a bad thing on the menu, though I can't say that I've tried it all.

Name: HG Supply Co
Description: Fabulous for a happy hour- their apps and cocktails are great! The patio is right on the Trinity River, so there is a lovely breeze on a spring afternoon.

Name: Quince
Address: 1701 River Run suite 181, Fort Worth, TX 76107
Description: I've been to a few birthday dinners here that were very well-executed, so a huge plus that they accommodate large parties well. The atmosphere is always lively, and it's a very nice touch that they have the Veuve Cliquot arrows for Fort Worth (it makes me unreasonably happy I can't explain it) and it on the menu. Though, I wouldn't necessarily order it instead of ordering a margarita, but to each their own.

Name: Asencion
Address: 1751 River Run Suite #151, Fort Worth, TX 76107
Description: LOVE this place for weekend brunch- make a reservation online the day before for no waiting! I've never had a bad meal here, but the service does remind me a bit of a Parisian café, where you have to flag down the staff to get anything. Not sure if that's on purpose or accident, but do keep it in mind. It's a slower pace, but great vibes. You can go after a workout, before shopping in Clearfork. or anything in-between.

Name: Luke
Address: 333 St Charles Ave, New Orleans, LA 70130
Description: Luke is on my dad's top-ten restaurants of all time list. I can see why. Their brunch is quintessentially New Orleans cuisine, and the atmosphere is amplified by the Marine sabers and medals on the walls. The wide windows let the sunshine in, and it's a place where you can't help but have a good time. If you're lucky, you'll go during their prix fix menu, and truly experience some of the best of the best that NOLA has to offer.

Name: Chuys
Address: 2401 W 7th St Suite 110, Fort Worth, TX 76107
Description: Yes, it's a Mexican chain, and no I have never had a bad meal here. Their creamy jalapeno is famous for a reason, and it's somewhere where you can't go wrong. If you go during peak dinner hours, be prepared to wait without a reservation, but honestly their lunch menu is pretty similar and just as delicious. They do seasonal beverages which are always incredible, and the frozen margaritas are perfect.

Name: Kincaids
Address: 4901 Camp Bowie Blvd, Fort Worth, TX 76107
Description: Kincaids is a Fort Worth institution. It's been around since it was a grocery store that happened to start selling burgers out of the back meat counter, and their first location still carries the nostalgia that would be found there. The burgers are fantastic, and I highly reccomend the ranch with the fries.

Name: Coco Shrimp
Description: Tucked into a corner of an industrial neighborhood, Coco Shrimp is serving up exactly what it sounds like: coconut shrimp. Of course, you can get grilled as well, but the coconut is so much more fun. There are six different choices for various preperations and additional sauces, and I haven't tried a bad one yet. A great atmosphere, and delicious.

Name: Eatzis
Address: 1540 S University Dr #100, Fort Worth, TX 76107
Description: While I've really only been to the sandwich line inside of this Italian supermarket, I have several friends who can vouch for the fantastic hot dishes that they also serve. All the bread is incredibly fresh, the meat is freshly sliced, and the large sandwich is as big as your head. A fantastic spot for lunch, and if you are lucky enough to grab a seat outside, you can enjoy Italian opera alongside the fantastic sandwich. Their iced tea is also phenomenal.

Name: Milk Bar
Address: 3708 Las Vegas Blvd S, Las Vegas, NV 89109
Description: Nestled in between Momofuku and a long winding hallway, this stand has not only fabulous lattes for the morning, but also some of the most interesting soft serve that I've ever had. It's cereal milk flavored, and comes with cornflakes around the side, and I've never encountered that unique of a combination, especially considering how accurate it is in description.

Name: é by José Andres
Address: Boulevard Tower, 3708 Las Vegas Blvd S Level 3, Las Vegas, NV 89109
Description: 6 seats, 4 chefs, and an unforgettable night. The menu changes extensively from night to night, and the chefs curate a menu that can last between 15 and 20 courses. Most memorably were the cotton candy empanadas with corn nuts, the freeze-dried Wonderbread and fois gras sandwich, and the charred octopus with rosemary air. This is a once-in-a-lifetime dining experience that takes into account dietary preferences, restrictions, and allergies to the highest degree.

Name: Velvet Taco
Description: Not only are their margaritas kick-ass, but so are their tacos and elote. It's open late, has a great rewards program, and convienently located in a few areas across the Metroplex.

Name: Secret Pizza
Address: Boulevard Tower, 3708 Las Vegas Blvd S Level 3, Las Vegas, NV 89109
Description: This place has no name. It's known only as "secret pizza" because it's hidden down a long and winding hallway where they're cranking out fresh pies left, right, and center. I can't even say there's something to order in particular here, because they make whatever pizzas they want at the time, though the white pizza was a repeat order for me.

Name: Prime
Address: Bellagio Hotel & Casino, 3600 S Las Vegas Blvd, Las Vegas, NV 89109
Description: I'm incredibly biased about my experiences at this restaurant. This is one of Jean George's places across Las Vegas, and the one that has been renovated the most recently. Soft blue velvet drapes, staff in tuxedoes, and gorgeous steaks on the menu, with regular bottles of my favorite (and very hard to find) bottle of champagne make this experience one to remember. I don't know if it's still on the menu, but I do dream about their lemon merangue pie a considerable amount.

Name: Jasmine
Address: Bellagio Hotel & Casino, 3600 S Las Vegas Blvd, Las Vegas, NV 89109
Description: If you're looking for authentic, upscale, traditional Chinese cuisine, this is the place to go. The dining is family style, and I recommend going in a larger group in order to accommodate the fact that you'll want to order everything on the menu. With a view of the Bellagio fountains and a laid-back attitude, this is a fantastic dinner before a show in Las Vegas.

Name: STK
Address: Boulevard Tower, 3708 Las Vegas Blvd S Level 2, Las Vegas, NV 89109
Description: Here, the dinner is the show. Past 10pm, there's usually a DJ in the corner spinning tracks, and it's a great place to go before heading to the Library next door at Marquee Nightclub. Don't skip the churros at the end- it was one of my favorite bites I've ever had.

Name: China Poblano
Address: Boulevard Tower, 3708 Las Vegas Blvd S Level 2, Las Vegas, NV 89109
Description: An interesting fusion of Mexican and Chinese cuisine, this restaurant focuses on the relationship that came out from the surprising mix of the cultures. Have some dumplings and 20 vegatable fried-rice (try to count them all!) alongside chips and a taco with some of the best Mezcal I've ever had.

Name: Joe's Stone Crab
Address: 3500 Las Vegas Blvd S, Las Vegas, NV 89109
Description: Go for the seafood or steaks, and stay for the peppermint ice cream. Seriously. One of the best Cosmopolitans in Las Vegas, and the overall experience is old school at its finest. It's worth mentioning how incredible their crab cakes are, too.

Name: Delmonico's
Address: 3355 S Las Vegas Blvd, Las Vegas, NV 89109
Description: Emril Lagasse has done it again. This would be one of the restaurants I am positive he is proud to have his name on the front of. The steaks here are of course phenomenal, but so is the staff and the atmosphere. It's the type of place where you know for sure that everyone loves working there and has a passion for the cuisine. The drinks are strong, the vibe lively, and the bacon appetizer is a fan favorite.

Name: Eggslut
Address: Boulevard Tower, 3708 Las Vegas Blvd S Level 2, Las Vegas, NV 89109
Description: It's totally worth it to stand in this line. Or, send your unlucky signifigant other to do it for you. The sandwiches are perfect, though it is worth noting that all the scrambled eggs are wet, if that's something that you care about. The hashbrowns are crispy on the outside and fluffy on the inside, and the fresh-squeezed orange juice is to die for. But, there's always a line.

Name: Reata
Description: This is one of the only places you'll find upscale wild game on the menu, though that is more common in Texas in general. There's a great location in Downtown Fort Worth, and some of the most well-produced Southern-flare fine dining I've ever had. Don't be put off by the things you've never considered eating before, and try something different.

Name: Wicked Butcher
Description: Don't be startled by the bone marrow on the menu, or the iPad which holds a very extensive wine list. Wicked Butcher is a modern take on a traditional steakhouse that feels sleek and sophisticated, because it is. A bottle of O'Shaughnessy pairs well with every steak on the menu, and there's no chance you'll leave hungry.

Name: Piola
Description: In a renovated old house on the corner of a neighborhood sits this family institution. There's not a bad thing on the menu, and I'd heartily recommend going with a group to try more things. The Grandma's Lasagna is the stuff of legend- you're practically possessed by a Nonna when you eat it.

Name: Murray's
Address: One Casino Drive, Suite 28, Paradise Island, Bahamas
Description: Murray's is some of the best breakfast on the island. The best part by far is the potato side to the omlettes and other eg dishes- something about the seasoning and the different veggies that are included in the sautee process are incredible. The environment is relaxed and perfect for easing into your vacation.

Name: Beatnik
Attire: Jeans and a going out top
Description: I went here with a dear friend of mine in the summer, and it is the perfect place to grab lunch on the riverfront, but it's even better for dinner. The atmosphere is lively, the dinner filling, and the drinks free-flowing. Part of the experience was the free shots from our waiter, but that's always a perk and not an expectation. The view is great and it's in the heart of downtown Chicago.

Name: Birdland Jazz Club
Attire: Jeans and a going out top
Description: Birdland is to jazz as Billy Bob's is to country music. It's one of those legendary venues that can kick-start a career lightning fast, and the cocktails are exceptional. You never know what you're going to get, but definitely get a t-shirt.

Name: Amalfi
Address: 3570 Las Vegas Blvd S, Las Vegas, NV 89109
Description: I'm a D-1 Bobby Flay hater, so the fact that one of his restaurants is on this list is a feat. I deeply dislike him as a person, but I can grudgingly admit that he has put together a great menu and atmosphere in this Italian and Mediterrenean restaurant. The crispy artichokes are incredible and a perfectly light starter to an otherwise hearty meal. The eggplant is pleasantly tender, yet not soggy

Name: Café Cliquot
Description: This is the perfect end to the tour of the Cliquot caves. I'd recommend spending a lot of quality time in the gift shop so the wait dies down a litte bit after the tour ends, and the burger is unexpectedly fabulous. When I was there, they had recently launched the Rich champagne, meant to be served over ice, so the couple of glasses of that helped. I wouldn't try to order any other kinds of wine however... you're at Veuve Cliquot, so drink it.

Name: Café Jade
Description: The breakfast here is perfect for the pre-shopping that's only ten minutes away at Le Bon Marche. Its reasonably priced, the tea and OJ are divine, and the croissants are warm. Can't go wrong here.

Name: L'ouzeri Paris Odéon
Address: 17 Rue Grégoire de Tours, 75006 Paris, France
Description: Y'all seen My Big Fat Greek Wedding? Dining in this restaurant feels exactly like being at one of those family reunions. The staff seems all somehow related, and the food is delivered with a unique family kindness that is hard to find elsewhere. It's comforting, especially in a country that was not our own. The food itself is pretty average, but the experience elevated it signifigantly.

Name: The Henry
Address: Boulevard Tower, 3708 Las Vegas Blvd S Level 1, Las Vegas, NV 89109
Description: Three words: tater tot nachos. Oh, and there's slow roasted short rib on top of them too. If you're there for breakfast, it's the same short rib that's on their eggs benedict and you're missing out if you don't get at least one of them. It's right across from a barbershop speakeasy in the Cosmopolitan, and they have some of the best breakfast cocktails this side of Vegas. A hangover can very easily be cured here.

Name: Beauty & Essex
Address: The Cosmopolitan of Las Vegas, 3708 Las Vegas Blvd S, Las Vegas, NV 89109
Description: There are several locations across the country, all with a similar vibe. It's a pawn shop in the front, where you can actually buy the things in the display case, but you give the person behind the counter a nod and a door with a huge guitar swings open to reveal the restaurant. Vegas has a lot of great themed restaurants, and this is one of them that dedicates themselves to the full enchilada. Speaking of enchiladas, the empanadas are absolutely fantastic- we never order any less than twice. The experience is tapas-style, so be prepared to order half the menu.

Name: Vesper
Address: The Chelsea Tower, 3708 Las Vegas Blvd S Level 1, Las Vegas, NV 89109
Attire: Jeans and a going out top
Description: This is the perfect bar to stop at before getting in line for a table at the club. There's great theming, unique cocktails, and it's less packed than the traditional Chandelier Bar that's a few steps away. It's perfect for people watching, as it's basically at the entrance, and the couches are quite comfortable.

Name: Javier's
Address: ARIA Resort & Casino, 3730 S Las Vegas Blvd, Las Vegas, NV 89109
Description: Another chain, I know, but Javier's is a staple for a reason. It's fun, it's high energy, and the Vegas location has the unique charm of being convienently located by several designer stores that are calling your name after a few margaritas. Thin chips, loud music, and good times are had here in excess.

Name: Sea Glass
Address: Paradise Beach Drive, Suite 34, Paradise Island, Bahamas
Description: Conviently located right as you're stumbling back to the hotel room, this bar feeatures a few slot machines and great music to wind down with a nightcap. There are a few lounge bites if you stop off on your way to dinner or another excursion, but keep in mind it's restricted to Cove guests until 7pm.

Name: Fish
Address: Paradise Beach Dr. Suite 15 Paradise Island N.P, Bahamas
Description: Jose Andres has done it again. Can you tell he's one of my favorite chefs? I love the Spanish flair that is brought to the fresh seafood at this restaurant, and it's the type of place where you can tell it's fresh. They have a great wine list with a few of my favorite bottles, and the thinnest glasses that my intrusive thoughts want me to take a bite out of. The perfect place to go after spending all day at the beach where you're slightly warm from the sun still.

Name: Graycliffe
Description: I know, we're venturing into town for this one. But it's a staple for a reason! One of the original fine dining restaurants, even pre-independence, Graycliffe is one of those places you need to visit once in your life. You don't need to stay there, but you do need to watch the sunset from the top of the hill with a crispy glass of Bahamian rum on the rocks with the slight breeze flowing through the trees.

Name: Waters
Description: Crab cake. Fish and Chips. Skillet Brownie. A perfect meal to share or all for yourself, the atmosphere is peak downtown Fort Worth. It's the perfect stop before heading to Thompson's Library or Pete's Dueling Piano Bar because it sets the tone so perfectly for a nice night out.

Name: Dutch's
Address: 3009 S University Dr, Fort Worth, TX 76109
Description: This is the perfect place for a quick bite to eat for lunch! Their buns on their burgers are surprisingly incredible, and their salads are always fresh. The atmosphere is a little younger, with an extensive bar to the side. It's the place to go to watch TCU football when it's an away game, and it should be with it being named after the famous TCU football head coach, Dutch Meyer. "Fight 'em 'til Hell freezes over, then fight 'em on ice."

Name: Hotel Particulier
Description: The brunch here is like stepping back into a time capsule. It's a prefix menu, where you pick your choices in each of the three categories, with of course additional things being available off the menu. The pancakes were clearly cooked in butter, as they had that tell-tale ring of crispy deliciousness around the edge, and the eggs benedict were divine. This botique hotel is in the old Hermés family home, with only restorations and not renovations. It's in the gorgeous Montmarre neighborhood, and a short walk  to Sacre Coeur and the rest of that idyllic scenery. Love!

Name: Le Sarah Bernhardt
Description: We happened upon this café when ducking to avoid the fat raindrops that were falling on one afternoon. The onion soup warmed us right up (or maybe it was the wine) and we had a fantastic snack here. As with most Parisian cafes, there is of course a lot more to the menu, but I can only speak in addition to the greatness of the tarte tartain. One of the best I've ever had.

Name: Lezard Café
Description: I was initially attracted to the greenery of the décor, but we stayed for the espresso martinis. There were food options, but we didn't order any because all we were looking for were drinks. The espresso was fresh, the martini glasses chilled, and the ambience was warm, even if the temperature was cold. Beware- as with most Parisian outdoor dining experiences, there are a lot of smokers and vapers.

Name: Bistro de Buci
Description: I'd definitely recommend dinner here over lunch, as the atmosphere is much more condusive to that. I adored the Boudoir cocktail, and they do in fact have Dissoronno Amaretto (bonus!) in addition to the French classics. Ask for a table outside, so you can watch the people go by in Saint Germain, but if you do sit inside don't get stuck at the table in the corner upstairs. We asked to move.

Name: Restaurant Les Arpents
Description: A phenomenal story from a very interesting restaurant. Two friends decided to open a restaurant where one of them did exclusively front of house, and the other was the chef. Each night, they cook you what they want you to have. The front-of-house guy talks to you a little bit, gets a feel for what you like and may be open to, and it's entirely a surprise what you recieve. Of course, any allergies or dietary restrictions are taken into account, but it is a fun and unique experience that's lurking in the streets of a quaint, storybook town.

Name: Le Raspail
Description: This café is right next to one of the universities in Paris, so be prepared for a lot of college-aged French at peak times. I just had drinks here, but the people-watching was outstanding and the atmosphere was young and hip. The table next to us really enjoyed their fries and salads, but we were on our way to dinner after so we did not partake.

Name: Josephine
Description: If something's been around since 1888, then they must be doing something right. Most weekends are reservation only, something strange for a French place, but it is so worth it. The duck is the speciality of the house (and the logo) but the scallops were also fresh and on a very interesting bed of leeks that made me want to try to cook it at home. The service was impeccable, though that could have been a fluke, and it is so worth going back to again. Quick storytime about how we found this place: a good friend of ours, her husband, and their son all went there and shared the whole duck for two. It was life changing for them. Her husband was in a terrible accident that left him in a hospital for months in Boston, and one of the things that his son said to him was that if he could get better, they could go back and have this amazing duck again. It's literally a life-saving duck.

Name: Cocorico
Description: I was reccomended this place by a dear friend who lived in Paris for a while when he was a kid. This was the place he and his family went to to celebrate with something "boujee" instead of cooking at home. It did not dissappoint. The classic French boeuf bourgignon was flavorful and rich, and the atmosphere inviting. There are a few locations across Paris, and I'd reccomend it wholeheartedly for a warm meal and a good cocktail.

Name: Pizzeria Popolare
Description: Make a reservation here, and the earlier the better. People start lining up outside about fifteen minutes before they open because this is a family-style seating place and once the first round of people come through, then you'll be sat with strangers. A fun concept, to be sure, but a little rushed. This is not a luxurious and languid dinner, this is eating good pizza quickly and moving to the next thing. Definitely reccomend for families with small children, though the limoncello spritz is perfect for the adults in the room.

Name: Ritz Paris
Description: There's a reason this place is famous. The Ritz is famous for their service, and for good reason. Even if you're just going for a drink at Hemmingway's bar, get there fifteen minutes before opening as there are no reservations and by 5:30 the wait was an hour and a half. If you're looking for something else, the tea service is excellent, though it is a French Tea Service, where everything is patisserie. Worth it just to be able to walk around this magnificent hotel.

Name: Vagenende
Description: I've been twice here, and gotten something equally as good but different both times. A good mix of tourists and locals, this brasserie has good vibes, and if you sit in the back there's a waiter who used to be an opera singer. You'll be able to tell which one he is- you can hear it across the restaurant. If you're not alone, the sole meunier is fabulous and prepared tableside, but it is the whole fish.

Name: Amorino
Description: I think this chain exists in the US as well, but these are dotted all along Paris. The gelato is the perfect snack as you walk leisurely back to your hotel, passing the nightlife and excitement of the streets, and it's reasonably priced.

Name: Maison Thevenin
Description: Go early in the morning for the warm and fresh croissants and baguettes. I know, everywhere in France has great bread, blah blah whatever, this place has a fantastic baguette that will be half eaten by the time you get where you're going.

Name: Patisserie Bigot
Description: The best tarte tartin that I've ever had. The family who owns this patisserie is the same as when it opened in the 17th century, and one of the oldest in the area. If you're looking for more than just a dessert, the omlettes are fantastic and so is the quiche. There's a great view of Chateau Montmarte as you're eating outside, which only adds to the surreal feeling of being in a once-medieval town.

Name: Chenonceau Bout
Description: A place to get a snack while getting the grand tour of the Ladies Castle grounds. If you go during the summer, they open up both sides, through the other side of the Loire River. It's about the atmosphere of stepping back in time, and oh so worth it.

Name: Chez Bruno
Description: The chef of this restaurant also has a family winery in the Loire Valley, something that is pretty common for the area. You can feel the love and care that is put into each dish, and the specials are always worth looking into.

Name: La Maison d'Isabelle
Description: Voted the best croissant in Paris… and they're right. Be prepared for the long lines, but oh my heavens it is worth it.
